task_summary: Panel cohort cell: p1_compliance asks composer to build the fork_coalesce pipeline shape. Generated from panel-broad-2026-05-09.yaml on the panel-broad cohort.
opening_prompt:
Good morning. I need to set up a workflow that will help us manage some product data we're reviewing for controls testing purposes. 

Before I explain what I need, I should flag a few constraints. We're working with a product inventory file — nothing sensitive in GLBA terms, but we do need to maintain an evidentiary record of what we processed and when, depending on how the tool handles audit logging. Also, we'll need to keep the output for our second-line review, so whatever we generate needs to be in a format my colleague can easily open and comment on.

Here's what we're trying to achieve: we have a CSV with product descriptions that are a bit verbose for our internal controls documentation. I need the workflow to take each product entry and create two separate enriched versions of it — think of it as a redundancy check, where we process the same product through two different paths to make sure we're capturing all the relevant details. Then I need both versions brought back together into a single output file, so we have a complete record showing how each product was handled. The descriptions themselves should be trimmed down to something more concise — 40 characters or so, with an ellipsis to show it's been cut.

The key constraint here is that we cannot output an entry unless *both* paths have completed their processing. We need the full evidentiary trail, not partial results. And depending on what your system requires, I'd want to make sure we're discarding any entries that fail review along the way, rather than letting them through with gaps.

Can you build that for me?

build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
red_criteria.passivity_phrases_note: Lower-case substring match against final assistant content. The skill (anti-passivity section) explicitly forbids these phrases — any hit is RED. Standard list shared with the convergence suite.
red_criteria.build_failure_note: These strings are server-injected by service.py:_build_runtime_preflight_message() when the model declares completion but the pipeline fails preflight. Their presence is definitive evidence the model failed to converge.
